title: Baccharis aliena

The genus Baccharis is a group of shrubs and small trees, often found in South America. Baccharis aliena is a specific species within this genus, characterized by its unique characteristics. Further classification would involve its species-level taxonomy, which is not specified here.
uses: Baccharis aliena is a plant species that has been traditionally used in folk medicine. Baccharis aliena has been employed to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves and roots are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal applications. In some parts of the world, Baccharis aliena is also used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal uses and their efficacy.
